definition: The OCF/revenue growth ratio with an approximate 1.0x threshold serves as a firm-level diagnostic for genuine vs reported commodity cost passthrough. Below 1.0x indicates reported margins overstate actual passthrough. S&P 500 Industrials showed ~0.4x during 2021-23 despite ~15% revenue growth.
evidence: Accounting identity: earnings = cash flow ± accruals, so cash conversion reveals real passthrough
S&P 500 Industrials: OCF/revenue growth ~0.4x during 2021-23 despite ~15% revenue growth
Pair_1 debate confirmed framework as directionally robust
open_questions: How should the 1.0x threshold be calibrated by sector?
Does the diagnostic work for services firms or primarily goods/manufacturing?
How do changes in trade credit terms affect the ratio interpretation?
